description: §8.5.1 INSERT + RETURNING (RUNTIME, sqlEngine:"real") — a write returns the written row + a follow-up SELECT reflects it. addNote runs `INSERT INTO notes (body) VALUES (${body}) RETURNING id, body`.all(); the real Bun.SQL engine assigns the autoincrement id (from the <schema> `id: integer primary key`) and RETURNING yields the written row {id:2, body:"second"}. countNotes then reads `SELECT COUNT(*) AS n`.get() → {n:2}, proving the write persisted. The stub returns [] for an INSERT (no FROM match) and, for COUNT, returns the raw seed row — so both assertions pass ONLY on the real engine.
rationale: §8.5.1: `.run()`/write returns void, but a RETURNING clause read via `.all()` returns the written row(s) — Bun.SQL executes RETURNING natively (verified). Fork B derive-from-<schema>: the `notes` DDL (`id: integer primary key`) comes from the case's <schema> via schema-differ generateCreateTable, so the autoincrement id is real (seed row id=1 → inserted id=2). The follow-up COUNT proves persistence within the run's single _scrml_sql instance. Two cells (@inserted, @total) are guard-rendered (`if=`) purely to consume them (avoids E-DG-002); the assertions are state-only.
